intro: The backbone is made of 33 small bones called vertebrae arranged in five regions.
notes: It supports the head and trunk.
It provides attachment for the ribs and pelvic girdle.
Between vertebrae are Cartilage Discs to absorb shock.

hotspots.label: Cervical Region
hotspots.description:
These are the 7 bones of the neck.

The first one is called the Atlas (supports the head) and the second is the Axis (allows the head to turn).
hotspots.examTip: The Atlas allows you to nod 'Yes', the Axis allows you to shake 'No'.

hotspots.label: Thoracic Region
hotspots.description: These 12 vertebrae correspond to the 12 pairs of ribs. They have long neural spines and facets for rib attachment.
hotspots.examTip: They are found in the Chest (Thorax) region.

hotspots.label: Lumbar Region
hotspots.description: These are the largest and strongest vertebrae because they support the most body weight.
hotspots.examTip: Lower back pain is usually caused by strain on the Lumbar vertebrae.

hotspots.label: Sacrum
hotspots.description: The Sacrum is a triangular bone located between the hip bones. It is made of 5 vertebrae fused together.
hotspots.examTip: It connects the spine to the Pelvis.

hotspots.label: Coccyx
hotspots.description: The Coccyx is the vestigial tail at the base of the spine. It consists of 4 tiny fused bones.
hotspots.examTip: It provides attachment for muscles but has no spinal cord inside it.
